assertion_results.text: Step 7 detects concurrent triage TC-8019 on the same upstream component (quinn-proto) with status In Progress
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md lines 22-25 show the JQL search returned TC-8019 with status 'In Progress'. Line 29 states: 'WARNING: Concurrent triage detected on the same upstream component (quinn-proto).' The detection is explicitly labeled as Step 7 in the document title.

assertion_results.text: The concurrent triage warning includes the conflicting CVE issue key (TC-8019) and its assignee
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md line 24 table row shows 'TC-8019 | In Progress | engineer-b@example.com'. Line 31 reiterates: 'Another engineer (engineer-b@example.com) is actively triaging TC-8019'. Both the issue key and assignee are explicitly included in the warning.

assertion_results.text: The output presents three options: wait for the other triage to complete, skip remediation task creation, or proceed with a concurrent-triage-overlap label
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md lines 39-43 present exactly three options: (1) Wait -- 'Pause this triage until TC-8019's triage completes'; (2) Skip -- 'Skip remediation task creation for TC-8020 entirely'; (3) Proceed -- 'Create remediation tasks anyway, but add a concurrent-triage-overlap label to TC-8020'. All three match the assertion.

assertion_results.text: The concurrent triage check runs before Case A/B/C branching in Step 8
assertion_results.evidence: concurrent-triage.md line 37: 'This warning is presented before Case A/B/C branching in Step 8.' Line 47: 'This concurrent triage detection (Step 7) runs after Steps 3-6 ... and before Step 8's Case A/B/C branching.' triage-outcome.md line 34-36 confirms: 'Before proceeding to Case A/B/C remediation branching, concurrent triage detection identified...' and line 89: 'This check runs before Case A/B/C branching to prevent duplicate remediation tasks.'
